## Definition

The nucleus is a membrane-bound organelle within the cell. Membrane proteins associated with the cell and its compartments include *ion channels*, *transporter proteins*, and *receptor proteins*.

## Core Principles

- *Ion channels* are proteins with pores through which ions can pass into and out of the cell via the process of *diffusion*.
- *Transporter proteins* actively transport substances into and out of the cell via *facilitated transport*.
- *Receptor proteins* recognize an extracellular signal, usually via the binding of hormones, which in turn alters intracellular functions.
